question.text: Which of the following is the correct chronological order of major derivative market milestones globally?

answer.text: CBOT forward contracts (1848) → CBOT futures (1865) → IMM currency futures (1972) → CBOE options (1973) → CBOT T-bond futures (1977)
CBOE options (1973) → CBOT futures (1865) → IMM currency futures (1972) → CBOT T-bond futures (1977)
CBOT T-bond futures (1977) → CBOT futures (1865) → IMM currency futures (1972) → CBOE options (1973)
IMM currency futures (1972) → CBOE options (1973) → CBOT futures (1865) → CBOT T-bond futures (1977)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CBOT facilitated forward contracts from 1848 and listed the first exchange-traded futures in 1865. CME's IMM launched currency futures in 1972. CBOE launched listed options in 1973. CBOT introduced T-bill futures in 1975 and T-bond futures in 1977. **The Trap:** Options and T-bond futures came after currency futures — many assume interest rate products came first because of their fundamental nature. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch1 provides this complete timeline. The chronology shows that commodity/agricultural derivatives preceded financial derivatives by over a century. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The evolution from commodity to financial derivatives reflects the increasing complexity of risk management needs — today financial derivatives dwarf commodity derivatives in volume.
